Scenario.overview: Communications Industry Complex CostaRicto group targets communications companies to disrupt operations and gain financial benefits.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) User Account File DNS Record Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email Delivery Artificial Intelligence Reg.exe Utility Process Creation (Mechanism) Delivery Method Code Injection Process Hollowing Scheduled Task/Job Application Log Content (Mechanism) SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking User Account Authentication (Mechanism) System Information Discovery User Account Authentication (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about potential targets using artificial intelligence.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies open ports and services on target networks by monitoring network traffic.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attacker creates custom malware for exploitation using software deployment tools.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted phishing emails to trick employees into revealing credentials.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker exploits vulnerabilities in web applications to gain access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker injects malicious code into processes to execute arbitrary commands.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-671: Requirements for ASIC Functionality Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attacker replaces legitimate processes with malicious ones to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to run at regular intervals for continued access.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker uses compromised credentials to escalate privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.technique_name: T1198: SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking
stage.description: The attacker disables anti-virus software to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker attempts to guess passwords using brute-force attacks.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about system configuration and network layout.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker uses valid accounts to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from compromised systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker transfers stolen data over alternative communication channels.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts critical systems to cause maximum impact.

Scenario.summary: The CostaRicto group conducts a complex attack against communications companies, starting with reconnaissance and resource development, then gaining initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and finally impact.
